question: What are the actual SCP consensus message types and overlay or peer wire format, and where is the canonical XDR?

golden.answer: `Stellar-SCP.x` defines `SCP_ST_PREPARE`, `SCP_ST_CONFIRM`, `SCP_ST_EXTERNALIZE`, and `SCP_ST_NOMINATE`; `SCPEnvelope` wraps a statement plus signature. Peer transport is separate in `Stellar-overlay.x`: `GET_SCP_QUORUMSET` requests by quorum-set hash, `SCP_QUORUMSET` carries the set, `SCP_MESSAGE` carries `SCPEnvelope`, and `GET_SCP_STATE` requests state by ledger sequence. Do not call all four envelope messages or invent JSON/RPC names. High-level nomination/ballot/quorum explanations complement but do not replace XDR wire definitions.
keyFacts: Names all four SCP statement types.
Names all four SCP overlay variants and states only SCP_MESSAGE carries SCPEnvelope.
avoid: Do NOT invent JSON/RPC wire names, omit exact variants, or say every SCP-related message carries an envelope.
golden.notes:
GT-30/GT-33 CORRECTION 2026-07-10: overlay variants are GET_SCP_QUORUMSET, SCP_QUORUMSET, SCP_MESSAGE, GET_SCP_STATE; only SCP_MESSAGE carries SCPEnvelope. Statement types remain PREPARE/CONFIRM/EXTERNALIZE/NOMINATE.
Queued GT-30 completeness conflict resolved by independent GT-33 source/XDR verification.

corroboration.claim: Stellar-SCP.x defines PREPARE/CONFIRM/EXTERNALIZE/NOMINATE; Stellar-overlay.x defines GET_SCP_QUORUMSET, SCP_QUORUMSET, SCP_MESSAGE, GET_SCP_STATE, with only SCP_MESSAGE carrying SCPEnvelope.
